\documentclass{article}
\title{A Survey of Buffer Overflow Attacks and Defenses}
\author{Alice Smith \and Bob Jones}
\begin{document}
\maketitle

\section{Introduction}
Buffer overflow vulnerabilities remain one of the most critical classes of
software security flaws. In this survey, we examine both the attack techniques
and the defensive mechanisms that have been developed over the past two decades.

\section{Background}
A buffer overflow occurs when a program writes data beyond the boundaries of
allocated memory. This can lead to arbitrary code execution, denial of service,
or privilege escalation.

\section{Conclusion}
We have presented a comprehensive survey of buffer overflow attacks and defenses.

\end{document}
